title: epipactis palustris f. albiflora
intro: Epipactis palustris f. albiflora is a rare, white-flowering variant of the marsh helleborine, a type of orchid native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which have been attributed to its bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids. The medicinal properties of Epipactis palustris f. albiflora are said to provide several health benefits, including reduced inflammation, improved immune function, and antioxidant activity, which may help protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are typically used in small doses to avoid potential side effects, and should be administered under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
